title: Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|title=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Jedi Power Battles''}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:Jedipower.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars'': Episode I
''Jedi Power Battles'' |developer=[[LucasArts]] |publisher=*[[LucasArts]] *[[Aspyr Media]] {{C|Re-release}}{{SW|url=news/star-wars-episode-i-jedi-power-battles-announce|text=''Star Wars Episode I: Jedi Power Battles'' Returns — Reveal}} |release date=*[[April 3]], [[2000]]{{SWArchive|url=gaming/videogames/news/news20000403.html|text=New Games Available Now}} *[[January 23]], [[2025]] {{C|Re-release}} |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=[[Wikipedia:Action game|Action]] |modes=*Single player *Multiplayer |ratings=[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]]: Teen (T) |platforms=*[[Wikipedia:Dreamcast|Dreamcast]] *[[Wikipedia:Game Boy Advance| Game Boy Advance]] *[[Wikipedia:Nintendo Switch|Nintendo Switch]] *[[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] *[[Wikipedia:PlayStation|PlayStation]] *[[Wikipedia:PlayStation 4|PlayStation 4]] *[[Wikipedia:PlayStation 5|PlayStation 5]] *[[Wikipedia:Xbox Series X and Series S|Xbox Series X/S]] |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer= |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer= |timeline=[[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |prev= |next= }} '''''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Jedi Power Battles''''' was a console game published for use with the Sony [[Wikipedia:PlayStation|PlayStation]] and Sega [[Wikipedia:Dreamcast|Dreamcast]] in [[April]] of [[2000]]. It inserted players into events in and during [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] from the perspective of one of five ''Episode I'' [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], such as [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]], and [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]]. Additionally there are number of secret characters that can be unlocked after completing the game such as [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]], and [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]. The Dreamcast version also included the Jedi [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]]. Players can play as [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] and [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]] in unlockable mini-games. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] appears as a NPC on Tatooine, Level 5. ==Gameplay== ===Playable Characters=== *[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] *[[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] *[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] *[[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]] (not on Game Boy Advance) *[[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] (not on Game Boy Advance) *Darth Maul (standard on Game Boy Advance, unlockable in others) *[[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]] (unlocked after finishing as Obi-Wan) *[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]] (unlocked after finishing as Plo Koon) *[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] (unlocked after finishing as Qui-Gon) *[[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]] (unlocked after finishing Training Mode with any Jedi - Dreamcast only) ===Levels=== There are fourteen levels, four of which are unlockable after completion of the main game ''except'' for the Game Boy Advance version. *[[Saak'ak/Legends|Trade Federation Battleship]] *Swamps of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] *City of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] *[[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|Theed Royal Palace]] *[[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] *[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]; this level is split into two sections *[[Gungan Sacred Place/Legends|Ruins]] *Streets of Theed (On a [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|STAP]]) *Palace Cliffs *The Final Battle *Droidekas - Assault Theed Palace as a Droideka (unlocked after winning as Plo Koon) *Kaadu Race - Race a [[Kaadu/Legends|Kaadu]] through the swamps of Naboo (unlocked after winning as Adi Gallia) *Gungan Roundup - Like conventional [[Wikipedia:Football|football]], with Jar Jar as the ball (unlocked after collecting hidden Gungan artifacts) *Survival Challenge - Defeat 100 enemies to gain the Ultimate Saber (unlocked after winning as Mace Windu) ===Enemies=== *[[B1-series battle droid/Legends|Battle Droid]] *[[OOM pilot battle droid/Legends|Battle Droid Pilot]] *[[OOM-series security droid/Legends|Security Droid]] *[[OOM command battle droid/Legends|Commander Droid]] *[[Flame battle droid|Flamethrower Droid]] *[[B1 grapple droid/Legends|Grapple Droid]] *[[Droideka/Legends|Destroyer Droid]] *[[Grapple droideka|Grapple Destroyer Droid]] *[[Plasma battle droid|Plasma Droid]] *[[Loader Droid]] *[[Kreetle]] *[[Slaatik hagworm]] *[[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|AAT]] *[[T4 turret droid]] *[[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] *[[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] *[[DRK-1 Dark Eye probe droid/Legends|Sith Probe Droid]] *[[Armored Krakkiss]] *[[Coruscant mercenary|Coruscant mercenaries]] ([[Kajain'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kajain'sa'Nikto]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]], [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]] and [[M'shento'su'Nikto]]) *[[Gungan/Legends|Gungan Warrior]] *[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] ===Platform differences=== There are several differences between gameplay in the PlayStation and Dreamcast versions of the game: *Ki-Adi Mundi is not in the PlayStation version, though his name still appears in the credits. *There is no difference in difficulty if you choose 'Jedi Mode' on the PlayStation version. *There is a training arena in the Dreamcast version only, which doubles as a two-player versus arena. *Various computer panels can be destroyed in the PlayStation version only to gain extra points. *If one chooses to play a two-player game, he/she will be unable to unlock all but the Survival Challenge bonus levels. *Power ups can be used in the PlayStation version. ==Continuity== ===Inconsistencies=== *If the player chooses to play as Queen Amidala during "Theed Palace", then she will not talk at all during the two cutscenes in the level. *Darth Maul has his own voice in "Theed Palace" but uses Mace Windu's voice in "Tatooine." *Qui-Gon for some reason is still alive after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]. *Mace Windu uses a blue lightsaber just like the Episode I action figure of [[Samuel L. Jackson]], as [[George Lucas]] had yet to agree that Windu uses the purple lightsaber seen in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Episode II]]. (His blue lightsaber is likely canon as the lightsaber hilt he uses also changes from Episode I to Episode II). *In "Final Battle" Darth Maul respawns at the energy bridge in the [[Plasma Refinery Complex|Theed generator room]] when defeated. If the player runs away and jumps down to the catwalk below the three rings another Darth Maul will be waiting there resulting in the player fighting two Darth Mauls at the same time. ==Legacy== {{Quote|Taking down battle droids has never been more fun. And, yes, Mace Windu has his purple lightsaber in hand this time.|The announcement post for the 2025 re-release of ''Jedi Power Battles''|}} On [[October 10]], [[2024]], a post on [[StarWars.com]] announced that a re-release of ''Jedi Power Battles'' based on the original Dreamcast source code and published by [[Aspyr Media]] would be released on [[January 23]], [[2025]]. The re-release will feature a number of enhanced features from the original version. This includes updates to the game's code to allow for gameplay improvements such as smoother jumps and enhanced balance. All unlockable characters from the original will be available immediately with a new roster of secret characters, including a pilot droid, Weequay, a droideka, and a loader droid. Players can also toggle between lightsaber colors from the original game and their [[canon]]-accurate versions for Mace Windu, Plo Koon, and Adi Gallia. ==Credits== {{Credits |cast= *[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] - [[Jake Lloyd]] *[[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] - [[James Warwick]] *[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] / Thug 3 - [[Scott Cleverdon]] *[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Queen Amidala]] / Handmaiden/ [[TC-14/Legends|TC-14]] Protocol Droid: [[Grey Griffin|Grey Delisle]] *[[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]] - [[Nancy Giles]] *[[Rugor Nass/Legends|Boss Nass]] / [[Peck]] - [[Greg Burson]] *[[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]] - [[Jess Harnell]] *[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] / Gungan Guard 1 /Thug 4 - [[Kevin Michael Richardson]]
